title: tephrosia egregia
intro: Tephrosia egregia, also known as the African milk plant, is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unique, milk-white latex. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Tephrosia egregia include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and burns. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia egregia can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's latex to create a topical ointment or cream.
